title: Latin continuous-cursive model (if open source + reviewer)
context: A continuous-cursive Latin model would extend the project's reach to classrooms and curricula that teach cursive writing alongside print. However, this task is explicitly blocked: it requires both an openly-licensed cursive formation source (not proprietary worksheet art) and a qualified cursive-formation reviewer — neither of which is confirmed. The project policy is to block (not fake): this task is not started until both are secured. It is listed to surface the dependency and invite contributors who can supply the open source and reviewer.
objective: Model the Latin continuous-cursive script (joined letterforms, joining strokes, A–Z uppercase and lowercase) as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef, using a gate-verified open font and with formation reviewer sign-off — conditional on securing both an open formation source and a cursive reviewer.
acceptanceCriteria: BLOCKER: An openly-licensed cursive formation source (not proprietary worksheet art) is identified, license-verified, and snapshotted per gate-002.
BLOCKER: A cursive-formation reviewer (qualified teacher or expert in cursive pedagogy) is named and confirms availability.
Latin continuous-cursive glyphs (A–Z uppercase and lowercase, plus standard joining strokes) modeled as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef.
Gate-verified open font for cursive forms sourced (gate-002 PASS artifact attached).
Formation reviewer signs the per-glyph checklist for joined letterforms and joining strokes.
Sheets render in A4 and US Letter; CC-BY-4.0 with attribution; provenance-gate green.
resources: PLAN.md section 9 M4, section 3 (non-prescriptive framing — no cursive vs print endorsement), section 16 Q3 (cursive open question)
gate-002 (provenance gate — must pass for both font and source)
output: data/latin-cursive/ directory with schema-valid cursive GlyphModel JSON files, SOURCES.md, reviewer-signed checklist, and trace-sheet PDFs — committed CC-BY-4.0. (Task remains blocked until open source + reviewer are secured.)
